name: SendGrid
one_liner: Twilio SendGrid is a cloud email delivery platform for transactional and marketing email via a REST Web API v3 and SMTP relay.
primary_auth: API key (Bearer token in Authorization header)
access_notes: Sign up gives instant, self-serve access with no human approval. As of 2025 Twilio retired the permanent free plan; new accounts get a 60-day free trial (100 emails/day) that includes REST API and SMTP access and working API keys. After the trial, paid Email API plans start at ~$19.95/month. Credentials are instant on signup, so buildable without payment during the trial window.
api_notes: SendGrid v3 REST API is extensive: Mail Send, dynamic templates, contacts/lists, marketing campaigns/single sends, stats, suppressions/bounces, subusers, sender authentication, alerts, and more (100+ endpoints). Official SMTP relay also available. Official SDKs in multiple languages (Python, Node, PHP, etc.).
mcp_notes: No vendor-shipped/hosted official MCP server found. Twilio published a developer blog tutorial on building your own SendGrid MCP server, and several community servers exist (e.g. github.com/Garoth/sendgrid-mcp, Pipedream). Composio also offers a SendGrid toolkit.
main_blocker: No hard gate for building: public REST API with self-serve API keys. Only caveat is the free tier is now a 60-day trial, so long-term production use requires a paid plan.

evidence.claim: SendGrid v3 API uses an API key sent as 'Authorization: Bearer <API_KEY>'; keys created in Settings, limit 100 per account.
Twilio retired the permanent free plan on May 27, 2025; new accounts get a 60-day free trial (100 emails/day) with REST API and SMTP access, then paid plans from $19.95/month.
Twilio blog is a tutorial to build your own SendGrid MCP server (not a vendor-hosted official server).
Community SendGrid MCP server exists for AI agents managing lists, templates, single sends and stats.

open_questions: Whether the 60-day trial still issues fully working Mail Send keys before sender/domain authentication is completed for all send types; classification could be SELF_SERVE_FREE vs SELF_SERVE_TRIAL depending on how the retired permanent free tier is weighted.
